purpose: Evaluate the full set of packages required for a complete, generic, enterprise-grade full-stack platform and determine which to add now, defer, or omit.
evaluationCriteria: Stable ownership boundary: a single team or role owns it unambiguously
Different dependency rules: it has forbidden consumers or dependencies distinct from neighboring packages
Different runtime/deployment concerns: it runs in a different process, environment, or has separate lifecycle
Different validation/evidence needs: it requires its own compliance or governance checks
Likely reuse: multiple other packages or teams will depend on it

packagesAdded.name: @platform/api-runtime
packagesAdded.rationale: HTTP server runtime is a distinct deployment unit with its own auth, middleware, and observability concerns. Separates API serving from domain logic and GraphQL server.

packagesAdded.name: @platform/graphql-api-runtime
packagesAdded.rationale: GraphQL server has distinct dependencies (schema stitching, DataLoader, persisted queries) and is consumed only by application packages — separate from the generic HTTP runtime.

packagesAdded.name: @platform/worker-runtime
packagesAdded.rationale: Background workers run in a separate process with different lifecycle (drain on shutdown), different dependencies (queue broker), and different operational concerns from HTTP servers.

packagesAdded.name: @platform/config-runtime
packagesAdded.rationale: Configuration is a leaf node with no @platform dependencies, consumed by every adapter and runtime package. Its typed-schema-at-startup pattern is distinct from any other package.

packagesAdded.name: @platform/session-runtime
packagesAdded.rationale: Session management crosses auth, Redis, and request context — distinct from security-auth (which defines the interface) and adapters-redis (which owns the connection). Security team ownership.

packagesAdded.name: @platform/security-auth
packagesAdded.rationale: Authentication abstraction is a pure interface with no @platform dependencies. Separating the interface from the provider (adapters-keycloak) enables provider swap without touching platform code.

packagesAdded.name: @platform/audit-events
packagesAdded.rationale: Audit event schema and emitter have distinct compliance ownership (security team), durable delivery requirements, and schema versioning governance separate from domain events.

packagesAdded.name: @platform/observability
packagesAdded.rationale: Observability interface (logs, metrics, traces) has no @platform dependencies and is consumed by every runtime and adapter package. Decoupling from vendors is a platform-wide concern.

packagesAdded.name: @platform/queue-runtime
packagesAdded.rationale: Queue topology contracts and enqueue interface must be a stable dependency point shared between producers (feature packages) and consumers (worker-runtime, adapters-redis).

packagesAdded.name: @platform/storage-runtime
packagesAdded.rationale: Object storage abstraction has no @platform dependencies and decouples feature packages from the S3 SDK. Presigned URL flow is a distinct pattern from other runtime concerns.

packagesAdded.name: @platform/adapters-keycloak
packagesAdded.rationale: Keycloak OIDC implementation is the concrete auth provider. Separating it from security-auth enables provider swaps and limits Keycloak SDK exposure to one package.

packagesAdded.name: @platform/adapters-redis
packagesAdded.rationale: Redis serves both cache (sessions) and queue (BullMQ) concerns. Consolidating both into one adapter is justified — both consumers are platform packages, not feature packages.

packagesAdded.name: @platform/adapters-sentry
packagesAdded.rationale: Sentry implements the observability error interface. Separating vendor SDK from the interface means error monitoring vendor can change without touching any other package.

packagesAdded.name: @platform/adapters-opentelemetry
packagesAdded.rationale: OpenTelemetry must be initialized before all other imports for auto-instrumentation — that startup concern is distinct from Sentry's error capture and warrants separation.

packagesAdded.name: @platform/adapters-object-storage
packagesAdded.rationale: AWS S3 SDK encapsulation belongs in a dedicated adapter. Presigned URL generation, multipart upload, and bucket policy are distinct from both config and storage-runtime concerns.

packagesAdded.name: @platform/dev-services
packagesAdded.rationale: Local development environment setup (Docker Compose, seeds) has local-only deployment scope and must never be a production dependency. Distinct from CI and infrastructure concerns.

packagesAdded.name: @platform/tooling-docker
packagesAdded.rationale: Container image definitions are a delivery concern with distinct ownership from infrastructure definitions (infra-aws) and CI workflow (tooling-ci).

packagesAdded.name: @platform/tooling-terraform
packagesAdded.rationale: Terraform workflow tooling (modules, provider locks, workspace scripts) is distinct from the actual infrastructure definitions (infra-aws). Shared module patterns are reused across environments.

packagesAdded.name: @platform/tooling-ci
packagesAdded.rationale: CI/CD pipeline definitions are the top-level delivery orchestrator — they invoke Docker builds, Terraform applies, and architecture governance checks. Distinct ownership from infrastructure.

packagesAdded.name: @platform/infra-aws
packagesAdded.rationale: AWS infrastructure definitions (ECS, RDS, ElastiCache, S3, IAM, VPC) are a delivery concern that changes on a different cadence from application code. Distinct from Terraform workflow tooling.

packagesDeferred.name: @platform/email-runtime
packagesDeferred.reason: Email sending is a narrow use case; can be an adapter within api-runtime or a thin wrapper initially. Revisit when multiple teams need it independently.

packagesDeferred.name: @platform/notification-runtime
packagesDeferred.reason: Push/in-app notifications require product-domain decisions about delivery targets. Premature to define generically before product requirements are known.

packagesDeferred.name: @platform/search-runtime
packagesDeferred.reason: Search infrastructure (Elasticsearch/OpenSearch) is product-specific. Deferred until search boundary is needed by more than one feature.

packagesDeferred.name: @platform/infra-cloudflare
packagesDeferred.reason: Multi-cloud CDN/edge is premature. Start with AWS-only (CloudFront). Add when the platform genuinely needs provider-level separation.

packagesDeferred.name: @platform/adapters-stripe
packagesDeferred.reason: Payment processing is product-domain specific. Not appropriate for a generic platform skeleton.

packagesDeferred.name: @platform/adapters-sendgrid
packagesDeferred.reason: Email provider adapter is product-specific. Deferred alongside email-runtime.

packagesOmitted.name: feature-flags (as standalone package)
packagesOmitted.reason: Feature flag reads belong in config-runtime. Insufficient boundary separation to justify a separate package at this scale.

packagesOmitted.name: data-migrations (as standalone package)
packagesOmitted.reason: Database migrations are tightly coupled to adapters-postgres schema ownership. Should live as a sub-module of adapters-postgres, not a standalone package.
